ew_context: Price completing wave 2 correction at Fibonacci 61.8% retracement level - classic Elliott Wave bounce zone for next impulse higher
pattern_desc: Fibonacci 61.8% bounce pattern confirmed at 101.37 level with RSI showing bullish momentum above 50%
invalidation: Close below 96.25 on D1 timeframe would negate the Fibonacci bounce setup
market_context: Middle East tensions and weaker dollar supporting oil prices amid growing US-Iran deal speculation
full_analysis:
## Market Structure & Technical Context

UK Oil has found support at the critical Fibonacci 61.8% retracement level at 101.37, forming a classic bounce pattern after declining from the 126.10 swing high. The EMA20 at 106.30 sits above EMA50 at 98.52, confirming the broader bullish trend remains intact despite the recent pullback. This Fibonacci level aligns with key support structure, making it a high-probability reversal zone.

RSI at 57.6% shows healthy momentum above the midpoint, while the 7.31 ATR indicates normal volatility conditions. The laminar flow pattern with momentum alignment supports the bullish bias.

## Key Levels

- **Resistance**: 110.81 (Fibonacci 38.2% retracement level and first target)
- **Support**: 101.37 (current Fibonacci 61.8% bounce level)

## Invalidation Level

Close below 96.25 on D1 timeframe would break the swing low structure and negate the Fibonacci bounce pattern.

## Macro & Flow Context

Middle East geopolitical tensions and dollar weakness are providing fundamental support for oil prices. CFD flow data shows Reynolds number of 14.37 with aligned momentum, confirming institutional interest at these levels.
